reference_answer: Study rooms may be booked for two hours at a time.
A group asking for a room during public service hours activates the rule.
The requester must hold a current library card.
Eight or more attendees defines a large-group booking.
Large-group bookings require staff approval before confirmation.
A public exam-prep session sponsored by a school, with the school sponsor named on the request, replaces staff approval with same-day notification to the branch manager.
Emergency closure notices override every room-booking rule.
The emergency closure notice has precedence.
Record requests in the blue room ledger.
